name: Annecy
summary: Annecy is one of France's safest cities, with very low crime rates and excellent safety infrastructure. This picturesque Alpine city consistently ranks among the top 3 safest destinations in France for travelers, with particularly high safety ratings for women and solo travelers.
quickVerdict: Annecy is remarkably safe for tourists, with crime statistics showing it ranks 181st out of 366 French cities for safety - placing it among the safest destinations in the country. Violent crime is extremely rare, petty crime is below national averages, and the city has excellent emergency services. The main risks are minor pickpocketing in tourist areas and standard travel scams. Women travelers consistently rate it as very safe, and public transport is reliable and secure.

neighborhoods.name: Vieille Ville (Old Town)
neighborhoods.description: The historic heart of Annecy with canals and medieval architecture. Well-patrolled and safe, though can get crowded during peak tourist season. Watch for pickpockets near major attractions.

neighborhoods.name: Centre-Ville
neighborhoods.description: Modern city center with shops, restaurants, and business district. Excellent lighting and police presence. Very safe for walking day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Lake Annecy Waterfront
neighborhoods.description: Popular area for tourists with beaches, parks, and lakeside promenades. Generally very safe but busy during summer months. Keep valuables secure at beaches.

neighborhoods.name: Seynod
neighborhoods.description: Residential suburb southeast of city center. Quiet and safe family area with good transport links. Very low crime rates.

neighborhoods.name: Annecy-le-Vieux
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential area with university campus. Extremely safe and well-maintained. Popular with families and professionals.

neighborhoods.name: Train Station Area
neighborhoods.description: Generally safe but requires normal urban caution, especially at night. Some petty crime reported but well-connected to police services.

scams.name: Petition Scams
scams.description: Individuals approach tourists to sign petitions for supposed charities, then demand donations or use the distraction to pickpocket.
scams.howToAvoid: Politely decline to sign anything and keep walking. Never engage with petition signers, especially in groups.

scams.name: Overpriced Tourist Services
scams.description: Some restaurants and services near major attractions may overcharge tourists or add unexpected fees.
scams.howToAvoid: Check prices before ordering, ask for menus with clear pricing, and be wary of places without posted prices.

scams.name: Fake Police Officers
scams.description: Rare but reported incidents of people impersonating police to check documents and steal valuables.
scams.howToAvoid: Ask to see proper identification, request to go to the local police station, never hand over passport or wallet on the street.

scams.name: ATM Skimming
scams.description: Occasional reports of card skimmers on ATMs, particularly at less secure locations.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks or well-lit areas, cover PIN entry, check for unusual devices attached to card slots.

soloFemale.overview: Annecy is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers, ranking as the 3rd safest place in France for women. Crime rates against women are very low, and locals are helpful and respectful. The city has excellent lighting, reliable public transport, and strong police presence.
tips: Stay in the well-lit city center and old town areas
Public transport is very safe even late at night
Locals are generally helpful if you need assistance or directions

nightSafety.overview: Annecy is very safe at night with well-lit streets, regular police patrols, and low crime rates. The city center and tourist areas remain active in the evening with good visibility and foot traffic.
tips: Stick to main streets and well-lit areas
Old town and city center are safest for evening walks
Public transport runs regularly until late evening

transport.metro: No metro system - Annecy uses buses
transport.rideshare: Uber and BlaBlaCar operate in the city and are considered safe and reliable
transport.taxis: Official taxis are safe and regulated, with set rates. All taxis must accept card payments
transport.tips: The SIBRA bus system is excellent and very safe. Consider getting an Oura card for convenient public transport access.

customs: Remove shoes when entering someone's home
Greet with 'Bonjour/Bonsoir' when entering shops or restaurants
Table manners are important - keep hands visible and wait for 'Bon appétit' before eating

health.overview: France has excellent healthcare standards. Annecy has good medical facilities though emergency response may be slower in rural areas around the lake.
health.water: Tap water is completely safe to drink throughout the city
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for France
health.altitude: Located at 448m elevation - no altitude concerns. Climate is temperate with warm summers and cold winters

faq.a: Yes, Annecy is one of France's safest cities for tourists, ranking 181st out of 366 cities for safety with very low crime rates and excellent emergency services.
Annecy is very safe at night, especially in the city center and old town which are well-lit with regular police patrols. Public areas remain active in the evening.

faq.q: Is Annecy safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Extremely safe - Annecy ranks as the 3rd safest place in France for solo female travelers with very low harassment rates and helpful, respectful locals.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Annecy?
faq.a: There are no particularly dangerous areas in Annecy, though exercise normal caution around the train station at night and be aware of pickpockets in crowded tourist areas.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Annecy?
faq.a: Yes, the SIBRA bus system is very safe, reliable, and well-maintained. Buses run frequently during the day and evening hours with good safety records.
